fields.name: input
fields.description: Input text to embed, encoded as a string or array of tokens. To embed multiple inputs in a single request, pass an array of strings or array of token arrays. The input must not exceed the max input tokens for the model (8192 tokens for all embedding models), cannot be an empty string, and any array must be 2048 dimensions or less. [Example Python code](https://cookbook.openai.com/examples/how_to_count_tokens_with_tiktoken) for counting tokens. In addition to the per-input token limit, all embedding  models enforce a maximum of 300,000 tokens summed across all inputs in a  single request.

fields.name: model
fields.description: ID of the model to use. You can use the [List models](/docs/api-reference/models/list) API to see all of your available models, or see our [Model overview](/docs/models) for descriptions of them.

fields.name: encoding_format
fields.description: The format to return the embeddings in. Can be either `float` or [`base64`](https://pypi.org/project/pybase64/).

fields.name: dimensions
fields.description: The number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have. Only supported in `text-embedding-3` and later models.

fields.name: user
fields.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help OpenAI to monitor and detect abuse. [Learn more](/docs/guides/safety-best-practices#end-user-ids).

types.name: CreateEmbeddingRequestInput
types.description: Input text to embed, encoded as a string or array of tokens. To embed multiple inputs in a single request, pass an array of strings or array of token arrays. The input must not exceed the max input tokens for the model (8192 tokens for all embedding models), cannot be an empty string, and any array must be 2048 dimensions or less. [Example Python code](https://cookbook.openai.com/examples/how_to_count_tokens_with_tiktoken) for counting tokens. In addition to the per-input token limit, all embedding  models enforce a maximum of 300,000 tokens summed across all inputs in a  single request.

types.name: CreateEmbeddingRequestModel
types.description: ID of the model to use. You can use the [List models](/docs/api-reference/models/list) API to see all of your available models, or see our [Model overview](/docs/models) for descriptions of them.

types.name: CreateEmbeddingRequestEncodingFormat
types.description: The format to return the embeddings in. Can be either `float` or [`base64`](https://pypi.org/project/pybase64/).

fields.name: data
fields.description: The list of embeddings generated by the model.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The name of the model used to generate the embedding.

fields.name: object
fields.description: The object type, which is always "list".

fields.name: usage
fields.description: The usage information for the request.

types.name: Embedding

fields.name: index
fields.description: The index of the embedding in the list of embeddings.

fields.name: embedding
fields.description: The embedding vector, which is a list of floats. The length of vector depends on the model as listed in the [embedding guide](/docs/guides/embeddings).

fields.name: object
fields.description: The object type, which is always "embedding".

types.description: Represents an embedding vector returned by embedding endpoint.

types.name: CreateEmbeddingResponseUsage

fields.name: prompt_tokens
fields.description: The number of tokens used by the prompt.

fields.name: total_tokens
fields.description: The total number of tokens used by the request.

types.description: The usage information for the request.

methods.description: Creates an embedding vector representing the input text.
